cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the options one by one. The question is asking which of the statements is true according to the passage. Option A: In Thailand people give fish and birds to the birthday person as presents. The passage says that in Thailand, they buy live fish and birds for the birthday person and then the person frees the animals, bringing good luck. So this is true, but let me check the other options. Option B: In Holland people give presents to unbirthday persons on their birthdays. The passage states that in Holland, children not only get presents but also give things out to classmates and teachers on their birthdays. So they give to others, but the statement says "unbirthday persons," which might not be accurate term from the text. The text doesn't use that term, but the action is giving to others. However, I need to check if this is what the option is saying. The option says "unbirthday persons," which is not a term used in the passage. The passage just says they give to classmates and teachers. So B might be incorrect because of the terminology. Option C: In Nigeria people who were born in the same period may celebrate their birthdays on the same day. The passage says, "In Nigeria, people in a certain age group celebrate their birthdays together, on a certain day." So "certain age group" suggests people born in the same period, and "celebrate their birthdays together on a certain day" matches the option. So C is likely true.
